The compatibility tool still calls the v1 create/update/get/delete operations unchanged, returns a structured warning, never creates a version automatically, and directs callers through iap_list_versions, iap_create_version when needed, and iap_upload_version_image. It retains the immutable snapshot, signed transfer, pre-commit rollback, terminal-state polling, and ambiguous-commit reconciliation contract.

inputs.localRole: Rollback only the known reservation for this upload attempt.

fields.localRole: Creates an immutable temporary snapshot before reserve, then derives reserve metadata, supplies binary upload bytes, and drives the commit checksum from that same snapshot.

tools.note: The handler snapshots the file before reserve; binary transfer through Apple's signed operations is a local/external step. COMPLETE is successful; FAILED is terminal failure; any other assetDeliveryState remains pending and is polled with conditional GET. After a confirmed 2xx commit, nonterminal processing is an accepted success with uploadCommitted=true, processingComplete=false, deliveryPending=true, and retrySafe=false. If PATCH transport remains ambiguous and reconciliation only shows pending, the outcome stays an error; either way the review screenshot is retained with inspection guidance. A mismatched 2xx commit resource triggers one GET of the expected screenshot ID; valid terminal or pending state is interpreted normally, while invalid reconciliation stays an error. A confirmed reservation that fails before PATCH is best-effort deleted independently of cancellation; an unresolved create response instead requires parent-collection inspection because Apple exposes no idempotency key for locating that exact reservation.
